items.author: https://www.chinatalk.media/feed
items.title: Will the MATCH Act Change Chip Controls?
items.textEn:
Starting in October 2022, the Biden administration’s Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) updated chip controls like clockwork, with a cadence of updates every year.

Since Trump 2.0, however, the clock has stood still. Export controls on semiconductors and semiconductor manufacturing equipment have not been substantively updated since December 2024. The Trump administration has been reticent to update controls and has even considered walking them back. Over the past year alone, there have been tiffs over allowing sales of the H20 and then the H200.

But the government has not been united in this stance, and Congress is now taking its own stab at export controls. In April, both the House and the Senate introduced versions of the Multilateral Alignment of Technology Controls on Hardware (MATCH) Act. If passed, the MATCH Act would serve as the legislative branch’s first major attempt to enshrine export control into law, a prerogative that has traditionally been the purview of the executive branch and its agencies.

The MATCH Act, which has already passed through the House’s Foreign Affairs Committee (HFAC), would carve export controls on semiconductor-manufacturing equipment (SME) into stone, meaning that America’s posture of chip controls would be almost immutable. This stands in contrast with the status quo of chip controls that can be changed at the whim of an administration. Given the MATCH Act’s potential for near permanence as an enshrined bill, it is worth exploring the bill in detail.

This article explores these questions:

· What does the MATCH Act do?

· What are the bill’s goals?

· How likely is the bill to actually accomplish those goals?

· What are the drawbacks and negative side effects of the MATCH Act?

· How does the MATCH Act affect geopolitics?

I also include some lagniappe at the end where I run the eval of seeing whether Opus 4.8 could’ve done all this analysis itself.

What Does the MATCH Act Do?

The MATCH has two main goals: (1) cement the core of existing export controls on SME and (2) harmonize allies’ actions with American rules.

The MATCH Act compels the executive branch to preserve export controls — both on equipment and against specific actors (certain Chinese companies or facilities) — that are on the books today. These include advanced deposition tools, wafer handlers, EUV and DUV lithography equipment, advanced testers, etchers, and metrology tools — all the usual suspects. The specific actors blacklisted include any Chinese advanced-node facility and the companies SMIC, CXMT, and YMTC. 1 The gravity of this rule is immense: if passed, no future presidential administration could unilaterally turn back the clock on these controls.

The most important part of the MATCH Act, though, is in the name: matching Dutch and Japanese export controls to American ones. The bill compels allied nations to adopt and enforce export controls with the same strictness that the U.S. does, something many in Washington feel allies have been slow to do.

The critical facet of this is the clause restricting the servicing of existing equipment in covered facilities. Currently, both the Netherlands and Japan allow their companies to service equipment in Chinese fabs, regardless of whether or not that equipment would now be considered prohibited for sale.

This is a major gap in export controls. Although American toolmakers like KLA, LAM, and Applied Materials are prohibited from such servicing, ASML and Tokyo Electron are still able to service advanced lithography, deposition, and etching tools in Chinese fabs known to be producing advanced-node semiconductors.

The U.S. has previously adopted stricter controls first and given grace to the Netherlands and Japan to synchronize controls in due time; Congress now thinks this grace period is over.

If allies do not harmonize their controls with America’s on tools and servicing within 150 days, the bill threatens to unilaterally impose such export controls via the Foreign Direct Product Rule (FDPR) . Because all these tools, from ASML’s lithography machines to TEL etchers, rely on some level of American technology, the U.S. will claim the right to restrict them itself. In theory, if ASML and TEL continue to service these tools in violation of the FDPR, the U.S. reserves the right to cut these companies off from American technology, impose fines, and even seize assets.

The House and Senate versions of the MATCH Act differ slightly, with the main difference being a matter of tone. The Senate focuses more on "positive incentives" to ensure diplomatic cooperation with countries like Japan and the Netherlands, whereas the House version seems more willing to force allies’ hands on the matter of export controls. In substance, however, the two versions are identical.

Potential Benefits

If the U.S. cements its controls and ensures allies similarly adopt and enforce them, it can extend an enduring advanced chip lead over China. By clearly demarcating the line of no return, Congress is establishing the minimum expectation for SME controls. Regardless of whether we sell China advanced chips or bargain with other leverage, the tools to make those advanced chips would officially be off the table for future administrations.

The Act’s largest potential for impact comes with restricting tool servicing. Maintenance and service for advanced lithography and etching tools is not an annual checkup but rather an involved and constant process. ASML and TEL maintain full-time staff stationed at fabs to conduct preventative maintenance, tool calibration, part swapping, and more functions that the fab could not operate without. Any time the tool is not running is precious time and money down the drain, and servicing is a crucial part of keeping tools running 24/7.

In theory, without the expertise of ASML and TEL engineers to replenish parts and fix tools, China’s most advanced fabs may grind to a halt. Some experts suggest that all these tools would be inoperable within a year. As soon as these tools go down, they will not be up and running again.

Fairness is another motivator for passing the MATCH Act. While allied companies reap unfettered revenue (as much as 25% of a company’s income) from servicing tools in China, American companies have watched from the sidelines. We have handcuffed our own companies under the assumption that our allies would follow suit, but our allies have not — at least not all the way. In the interest of national security and commercial interests, we must level the playing field.

Potential Drawbacks

Good reasons also exist to believe that the MATCH Act will not achieve its mission and may even lead to unintended consequences.

Chinese Engineers Can Do the Job

In time, China may be able to service equipment themselves and simply resort to domestic alternatives for parts. For current service and maintenance, companies like TEL and ASML have offices across China. Their field service engineers (FSEs) are Chinese engineers, who will not pack their bags and leave even while their companies do. Both TEL and ASML hire candidates directly for work in China, instead of dispatching Japanese or Dutch FSEs to Chinese fabs. Hiring field service engineers as subcontractors, rather than direct employees, is also common practice in the industry, and such subcontractors would inevitably be Chinese companies. 2 If TEL and ASML leave, the engineers who fixed their machines will still be around to fix them, now at a premium salary. Some engineers, particularly at ASML, may be dispatched from the Netherlands or other geographies for certain specialized maintenance, but the reality is that the fabs in China will not screech to a halt due to the MATCH Act.

Chinese Companies Can Replace (Some of) the Parts

The greatest struggle for China would likely not be maintenance, but rather replenishing parts. Lithography, deposition, and etching tools are constantly requiring part swapping because gases become expended or something breaks down. If TEL and ASML leave, how will these tools receive their parts?

My understanding of how this plays out is that no one has an understanding of how it will play out. Take an ASML DUV lithography machine for example. It is the job of at least one ASML engineer to replace an excimer laser when it is exhausted. The company that provides these excimer lasers isn’t ASML, but usually an authorized vendor like ASML-owned Cymer or Japan’s Gigaphoton. If the MATCH Act passes, will Cymer and Gigaphoton still deliver excimer lasers even if ASML isn’t able to touch the machine? The language of the bill is vague enough to leave it up for interpretation. The result would depend on the strength of new Japanese and Dutch controls or on the American executive branch’s enforcement of the bill via the FDPR. Most likely, the administration would defer to the status quo and not try to enforce beyond the bare minimum.

Even if Gigaphoton and Cymer cannot touch the machine, mainland Chinese alternatives may throw a wrench into Congress’s plans. A company like Beijing RSLaser could come to replace the machine’s light sources and excimer lasers. Along the same vein, third-party unauthorized vendors for tool parts are rampant in the semiconductor industry. Someone somewhere leaks a part design to a company that wasn’t supposed to have it, and that company can sell the part even if its competitors are prohibited from doing so.

Besides maintaining and replacing tools, though, the MATCH Act could accelerate China’s use of indigenous tools. This is more of a concern for etching and deposition tools, as Chinese capabilities are already at or near the frontier required for advanced chip production. Perhaps Chinese machines would have worse throughput, and the country would also struggle to produce enough machines to replace fleets of TEL equipment overnight, but the technical capability is there.

Where it is not is lithography machines. If ASM…

items.author: Sergey Levine
items.authorName: Sergey Levine · 机器人学习（UC Berkeley）
items.title: SC3-Eval: Evaluating Robot Foundation Models via Self-Consistent Video Generation
items.textEn:
SC3-Eval: Evaluating Robot Foundation Models via Self-Consistent Video Generation

Evaluating generalist robot manipulation policies in the real world is expensive, slow, and difficult to scale. Action-conditioned video world models offer a scalable alternative by simulating policy rollouts. Autoregressive rollouts accumulate compounding errors, observations across multiple camera views must remain mutually consistent, and the evaluator must generalize to policies whose behaviors lie outside the training distribution. We address these challenges with SC3-Eval, a self-consistent video generation recipe that adapts a pre-trained video foundation model into an accurate policy evaluator by enforcing three complementary forms of consistency. First, forward-inverse dynamics consistency jointly trains the model to predict frames from actions and to recover actions from frames, anchoring generated rollouts to a physically plausible action manifold and counteracting the drift a forward-only model cannot penalize. Second, cross-view consistency trains the model to inpaint each camera view from the other, keeping the multi-camera observation coherent over long rollouts without any explicit memory mechanism. Third, test-time consistency reuses the inverse dynamics mode at inference as a per-action-chunk uncertainty signal that terminates rollouts whose generated frames drift away from the requested actions. We also demonstrate SC3-Eval rollouts reproduce the failure modes that policies exhibit in real-world rollouts, supporting fine-grained diagnostic comparison rather than aggregate ranking alone. Across seven real-world vision-language-action policies, SC3-Eval attains a closed-loop Pearson correlation of $0.929$ and MMRV of $0.119$, outperforming three strong prior video-model-based baselines, and generalizes to new tasks.
items.summaryZh: 文章介绍了SC3-Eval，一种通过自洽视频生成评估机器人通用操作模型的方法，通过前向‑逆动力学、一致视角和测试时一致性三种机制，提高了模拟滚动的物理真实性和跨摄像头一致性，并在七种真实视觉‑语言‑动作策略上实现了高相关性评估，帮助投资者了解机器人基础模型评估技术的进展及其商业潜力。
items.translationZh:
SC3-Eval：通过自洽视频生成评估机器人基础模型

在真实环境中评估通用机器人操作策略成本高、速度慢且难以规模化。条件动作视频世界模型提供了一种通过模拟策略滚动进行可扩展评估的替代方案。自回归滚动会累积复合误差，多个摄像头视角的观测必须保持相互一致，评估者还必须能够推广到行为超出训练分布的策略。我们通过SC3-Eval解决了这些挑战，这是一种自洽视频生成方案，通过强制三种互补的一致性，将预训练视频基础模型适配为精准的策略评估器。

首先，前向‑逆动力学一致性同时训练模型从动作预测帧并从帧恢复动作，将生成的滚动锚定在物理上合理的动作流形上，抵消仅前向模型无法惩罚的漂移。第二，跨视角一致性训练模型从另一摄像头视角填补每个视角，使多摄像头观测在长滚动过程中保持连贯，而无需显式记忆机制。第三，测试时一致性在推理阶段复用逆动力学模式，将其作为每段动作的“不确定性信号”，终止那些生成帧偏离所请求动作的滚动。

我们还展示，SC3-Eval的滚动能够再现真实滚动中策略出现的失效模式，支持细粒度的诊断性比较，而不仅是整体排序。针对七种真实世界的视觉‑语言‑动作策略，SC3-Eval实现了0.929的闭环皮尔逊相关系数和0.119的MMRV，优于三种强基准视频模型，并能够推广到新任务。

items.author: https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UCXl4i9dYBrFOabk0xGmbkRA
items.title: Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer
items.textEn:
Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer

Had Ada Palmer back on – this time to talk about Machiavelli, perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time.

Machiavelli cut his teeth as a high-level diplomat for Florence, a position from which he got to closely observe the most important rulers in Europe at the time, including the ones who were on the path to destroying his dearly beloved Florence.

In 1513 the Medici retook control of Florence and, wrongly suspecting Machiavelli of participating in a coup attempt, fired, tortured, and exiled him. Machiavelli could have fled his exile and worked for any number of different principalities that would have been eager to make use of his talents.

Instead, he decided to rot in the countryside and compile his career’s lessons about power, politics, and human nature into a book he dedicated to the very man whose new regime had tortured and exiled him, Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici.

But at least the Medici were in a position to use his insights to defend Florence. Machiavelli the patriot did not want any other hands to touch this book, because those hands, armed with these lessons, might pose an existential danger to Florence.

The closest modern analogy, at least as Machiavelli would have seen it, would be Szilard’s letter warning FDR about the possibility of a nuclear fission bomb.

What were those insights? And how were they inspired by Machiavelli’s  dangerous diplomatic missions all across Europe, and his extensive reading of antiquity? Watch this episode with Ada Palmer to find out!

By the way, Ada is launching a new podcast which I’m very excited about. The first season will be about Machiavelli – a perfect way to dive deeper into the topics we discussed in this episode.

Subscribe at Beforecast’s website to be notified of the first episode: https://beforecast-seven.vercel.app/
Subscribe on YouTube: @Beforecast 
Follow her on Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/adapalmer
Check out her FixTheNews Podcast episode: https://savingtheworldpod.transistor.fm/
And if you want even more Ada check out her books and more: https://www.adapalmer.com/


𝐄𝐏𝐈𝐒𝐎𝐃𝐄 𝐋𝐈𝐍𝐊𝐒
* Transcript: https://www.dwarkesh.com/p/ada-palmer-2
* Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/ada-palmer-machiavelli-is-the-most-misunderstood/id1516093381?i=1000772996754
* Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/episode/1Wx4gaPNEuauMODv8W2XTA?si=Bzfl_SSvSXCr4Xh4u2Q6lg

When a video file from a shoot came out corrupted, I pointed Cursor at it: it recovered the footage on its own, tracking down the right reference file from the file’s metadata and realigning the out-of-sync audio. My whole team now uses Cursor for everyday tasks, not just coding. Get started at https://cursor.com/dwarkesh

* Jane Street’s hiring process has been going viral on Twitter lately. The memes are pretty funny, but I wanted to see what their interviews were actually like. So I had Ricson, one of Jane Street’s ML researchers, walk me through a retired puzzle: he gave me an image dataset where 50% of the files had been corrupted – I had to figure out how to recover them. If you’re interested in these sorts of puzzles, you can find Jane Street’s open roles at https://janestreet.com/dwarkesh

* Crusoe is turning the AI datacenter buildout into an industrial process. At their massive Colorado factory, they assemble Spark units, modular datacenters with power, cooling, and fire suppression built in. They also manufacture specific components in-house to skip the longest lead times. Crusoe has experience running these Spark units on a range of energy sources, including solar and used EV batteries, ensuring they don’t get bottlenecked by grid availability. Learn more at https://crusoe.ai/dwarkesh

To sponsor a future episode, visit https://dwarkesh.com/advertise.

items.summaryZh: Ada Palmer 讨论了马基雅维利作为佛罗伦萨外交官的经历以及他被流放后写《君主论》的动机，强调这本书是为防御佛罗伦萨免受外部侵略而写，对理解权力、政治与人性具有重要意义。
items.translationZh:
Machiavelli is the most misunderstood thinker of all time – Ada Palmer

Had Ada Palmer back on – this time to talk about Machiavelli, perhaps the most misunderstood thinker of all time.

Machiavelli cut his teeth as a high-level diplomat for Florence, a position from which he got to closely observe the most important rulers in Europe at the time, including the ones who were on the path to destroying his dearly beloved Florence.

In 1513 the Medici retook control of Florence and, wrongly suspecting Machiavelli of participating in a coup attempt, fired, tortured, and exiled him. Machiavelli could have fled his exile and worked for any number of different principalities that would have been eager to make use of his talents.

Instead, he decided to rot in the countryside and compile his career’s lessons about power, politics, and human nature into a book he dedicated to the very man whose new regime had tortured and exiled him, Lorenzo di Piero de’ Medici.

But at least the Medici were in a position to use his insights to defend Florence. Machiavelli the patriot did not want any other hands to touch this book, because those hands, armed with these lessons, might pose an existential danger to Florence.

The closest modern analogy, at least as Machiavelli would have seen it, would be Szilard’s letter warning FDR about the possibility of a nuclear fission bomb.

What were those insights? And how were they inspired by Machiavelli’s  dangerous diplomatic missions all across Europe, and his extensive reading of antiquity? Watch this episode with Ada Palmer to find out!

By the way, Ada is launching a new podcast which I’m very excited about. The first season will be about Machiavelli – a perfect way to dive deeper into the topics we discussed in this episode.

And if you want even more Ada check out her books and more: https://www.adapalmer.com/

items.author: Chelsea Finn
items.title: Improving Robotic Generalist Policies via Flow Reversal Steering
items.textEn:
Improving Robotic Generalist Policies via Flow Reversal Steering

Generalist policies can learn a wide range of skills from diverse robot datasets. In order to solve or improve on challenging new tasks, we need a way to infer and invoke the appropriate actions from the policy's rich behavioral prior, especially when directly commanding the policy fails. We focus on flow matching generalists and propose Flow Reversal Steering (FRS): a method that takes suboptimal but ``reasonable'' actions, finds their latent noises by passing them through the flow policy in reverse, and maps them to nearby generalist action modes. We evaluate FRS across many simulated and real-world manipulation settings. First, FRS can turn coarse semantic guidance from humans or vision-language models (VLMs) into corresponding good robot actions, improving zero-shot control. These gains can be distilled with behavioral cloning by training an auxiliary policy to output noises that the generalist maps to good actions -- showing up to 95% absolute task success rate boosts in under a minute of training. Finally, FRS enables policy improvement by bootstrapping reinforcement learning with semantic knowledge, improving on several tasks that standard RL fails to improve on.
items.summaryZh: 文章提出了流逆转引导（FRS）方法，利用通用机器人策略的潜在噪声将次优但合理的动作映射到更好的动作模式，从而提升零样本控制和强化学习的任务成功率，对投资者关注机器人自动化技术的潜在提升路径具有重要意义。
items.translationZh:
Improving Robotic Generalist Policies via Flow Reversal Steering

通用策略（generalist policies）可以从多样化的机器人数据集学习广泛的技能。为了在挑战性新任务上取得突破或改进，我们需要一种方法来推断并调用策略丰富的行为先验，尤其是在直接指令策略失效时。我们聚焦于流匹配（flow matching）通用模型，提出了流逆转引导（Flow Reversal Steering，FRS）：该方法接受次优但“合理”的动作，通过逆向传递这些动作到流策略中，寻找其潜在噪声（latent noises），并将其映射到附近的通用动作模式。

我们在多种模拟和真实世界的操作场景中评估了FRS。首先，FRS 能将来自人类或视觉语言模型（Vision‑Language Models，VLMs）的粗糙语义指导转化为相应的优质机器人动作，提升了零样本控制的效果。这些提升可以通过行为克隆（behavioral cloning）进行蒸馏：训练一个辅助策略输出噪声，使通用模型映射到好的动作——在不到一分钟的训练时间内，任务成功率最高提升了 95% 的绝对值。

最后，FRS 通过引导强化学习（reinforcement learning）结合语义知识，实现了策略的改进，在若干任务上取得了标准RL 无法提升的效果。

items.author: Chelsea Finn
items.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
items.textEn:
DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?

Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are increasingly deployed as high-level planners for embodied agents, with an emerging strategy of scaling test-time compute to improve capability. However, we observe that doing so increases latency, token usage, and FLOPs while yielding uneven, often diminishing gains in downstream success, limiting where embodied agents can be deployed. We argue that choosing when and where to spend test-time compute is central to bringing frontier performance to the real world. We introduce DIRECT, a routing framework that uses multimodal scene context to allocate compute per prompt, improving the success--cost Pareto frontier over fixed model selection. Across three dominant scaling axes, namely chain-of-thought depth, model size, and memory history, our experiments on VLABench and RoboMME show that test-time compute is not a uniform lever: different axes yield qualitatively distinct capability gains. We validate these insights on a physical Franka arm in a DROID setup spanning zero-shot manipulation and long-horizon chaining, where our router matches or exceeds a stronger model's success rate at up to 65% lower average latency. Ultimately, our results show that naively scaling test-time compute is wasteful, and that DIRECT can provide frontier-level embodied planning in robotic systems at a fraction of the cost. Project page can be found at jadee-dao.github.io/direct/.
items.summaryZh: 文章介绍了在具身规划（机器人任务）中，直接使用测试时计算资源的时机和位置对提升实际性能至关重要，提出了通过多模态场景上下文进行计算分配的 DIRECT 框架，以在成功率和成本之间实现更优的权衡。
items.translationZh:
标题：DIRECT：何时何地在具身规划中分配测试时计算资源？

正文：DIRECT：何时何地在具身规划中分配测试时计算资源？

视觉语言模型（Vision-Language Models，VLM）正日益被用作具身代理的高层规划器，出现了一种通过扩大测试时计算来提升能力的策略。然而，我们观察到，这样做会增加延迟、令牌使用量和 FLOPs（每秒浮点运算次数），并且在下游成功率上带来的收益往往不均衡、甚至递减，限制了具身代理的部署场景。我们认为，选择何时以及在何处使用测试时计算是将前沿性能落地的关键。

我们推出了 DIRECT，这是一种路由框架，利用多模态场景上下文在每个提示（prompt）上分配计算，从而在固定模型选择的基础上改进成功率—成本的帕累托前沿。围绕三个主要的尺度扩展维度——思路链（chain‑of‑thought）深度、模型规模和记忆历史——我们的实验在 VLABench 和 RoboMME 上表明，测试时计算并非统一的杠杆：不同维度带来的是性质上截然不同的能力提升。

我们在 DROID 设置下的实体 Franka 手臂上验证了这些洞见，覆盖零样本操作和长时序链式任务，结果显示我们的路由器在成功率上匹配或超越更强模型，同时平均延迟降低最高可达 65%。

最终，研究表明，盲目扩大测试时计算是一种浪费，而 DIRECT 能以更低成本为机器人系统提供前沿水平的具身规划能力。项目页面见 jadee‑dao.github.io/direct/。

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: A Functional Taxonomy of World Models
items.textEn:
Renderers, Simulators, Planners, and the Loop That Connects Them

In an earlier essay , we argued that spatial intelligence is AI’s next frontier and that world models are the path to it. Here, the World Labs team and I want to go one level deeper: of the many things now being built and called ‘world models,’ which functional pieces actually compose that capacity — and what is each one for?

Language models have given machines an extraordinary command of concepts, vocabulary, and reasoning, but the physical world, virtual or real, runs on a different substrate. Where language models learn the statistical structure of text, world models learn the statistical structure of space and time: how light falls on a surface, how a garden looks from an angle no camera has captured, how objects respond to force and follow the laws of physics.

That makes “world model” one of the most important and most overloaded terms in AI today. Computer vision, robotics, reinforcement learning, and generative AI each claim to be building world models, and each means something quite different. A video model that produces gorgeous but physically impossible flames, a language model improvising a playable game, and a physics engine that faithfully simulates combustion all go by the same name.

The ancient Greeks could never agree on what the world was made of, whether fire, water, or indivisible atoms, because “world” was never a single thing. It was always a stand-in for whatever totality a given thinker needed to reason about. AI has inherited the same problem, at exactly the moment when the field needs precision.

Cutting through that confusion starts with a diagram older than any of the technology in question. Reinforcement learning textbooks, including the canonical Sutton and Barto, have used a version of the same picture for decades to describe how an agent interacts with a world. The formal name for this picture is the partially observable Markov decision process, or POMDP, and the original definition of the term “world model” belongs to that tradition.

An agent, which can be a person, a robot, or a software system, takes actions. Those actions affect the state of the world. The agent never sees the state directly. What reaches the agent are observations: the photons that fall on a retina, the readings from a sensor, and the pixels in a video frame. New observations inform new actions, and the loop continues.

The word “state” needs unpacking, because the meaning shifts from field to field. This is not the chemist’s state, the difference between solid, liquid, and gas. This is the physicist’s and roboticist’s state: a complete description of what is happening in the world at a given moment, including every object, every position, every velocity, every property. State is the underlying reality of the world; complete in principle, but never directly visible to any agent inside it. Observations are an agent’s partial view of that reality. Actions are what the agent does in response.

This loop — agent to action to state to observation and back — is the structure that gave the modern term “world model” its technical meaning. The phrase itself is older, traced to Kenneth Craik’s 1943 proposal that minds reason by running “small-scale models” of reality, and carried into neural networks by the late 1980s and early 1990s. And the loop also explains what people mean by the term today. The different things now being called world models are in fact different projections of this same loop. Each one outputs a different piece of it.
…
items.summaryZh: 文章阐述了“世界模型”的概念来源及其构成要素，指出它来源于强化学习中的POMDP框架，核心是智能体、动作、状态、观察的循环，对投资者而言把握这一技术框架有助于理解相关AI产业的真正价值与应用前景。
items.translationZh:
标题：世界模型的功能分类

正文：渲染器、模拟器、规划器以及将它们连接起来的循环

在之前的一篇文章中，我们认为空间智能是AI的下一个前沿，而世界模型是实现它的路径。在这里，World Labs团队和我想更进一步：在当前被称为‘世界模型’的众多技术中，究竟哪些功能部件真正构成了这种能力——每个部件的作用是什么？

语言模型赋予机器对概念、词汇和推理的非凡掌控，但物理世界——无论是虚拟的还是现实的——运行在完全不同的基质上。语言模型学习文本的统计结构，而世界模型学习空间和时间的统计结构：光如何落在表面上，花园从未被相机捕捉的角度会是什么样子，物体如何响应力并遵循物理定律。

这使得‘世界模型’成为当今AI领域最重要且最被滥用的术语之一。计算机视觉、机器人、强化学习和生成式AI都声称在构建世界模型，而它们各自指的东西却大相径庭。一个生成华丽却物理上不可能的火焰的视频模型、一个即兴创作可玩游戏的语言模型、以及一个忠实模拟燃烧的物理引擎，都被冠以同一个名字。

古希腊人永远无法就世界的本质达成一致——是火、水，还是不可分割的原子——因为‘世界’本身从来就不是单一的东西。它始终是思考者为进行推理所需的整体的代称。AI在正需要精确的时候，也继承了同样的问题。

要切开这种混乱，需要回到比任何当代技术都更早的图示。强化学习教材，包括经典的Sutton 与 Barto，多年来一直使用同一张图来描述智能体如何与世界交互。这张图的正式名称是部分可观测马尔可夫决策过程（POMDP），而‘世界模型’这一术语的最初定义正源于该传统。

智能体可以是人、机器人或软件系统，它会执行动作。动作会影响世界的状态。智能体从未直接看到状态本身。到达智能体的是观察：落在视网膜上的光子、传感器的读数以及视频帧中的像素。新的观察驱动新的动作，循环往复。

‘状态’一词需要拆解，因为其含义在不同领域会转变。这不是化学家的‘状态’（固体、液体、气体），而是物理学家和机器人学家的‘状态’：对某一时刻世界中发生的一切的完整描述，包括每个对象、每个位置、每个速度、每个属性。状态是世界的底层现实，原则上是完整的，但对任何内部智能体而言永不可直接感知。观察是智能体对该现实的部分视图。动作是智能体对观察的响应。

这个循环——智能体→动作→状态→观察→返回智能体——构成了赋予现代‘世界模型’技术含义的结构。该词本身更早可追溯到Ken​nth Craik 1943年的提议——认为心智通过运行‘小规模模型’来推理现实——并在20世纪80、90年代晚期被引入神经网络领域。而这个循环也解释了当今人们对该术语的理解。如今被称为世界模型的不同事物，实际上是同一循环的不同投射。每一种都输出该循环的不同部分。

items.author: https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UC5xLV_gJAP9psKcyrJ3ZIcw
items.title: Danfei Xu：人类数据，行为克隆，机器人GPT-3，全栈，EgoMimic，遥操作，UMI，斯坦福 | WhynotTV Podcast #5
items.textEn:
Danfei Xu：人类数据，行为克隆，机器人GPT-3，全栈，EgoMimic，遥操作，UMI，斯坦福 | WhynotTV Podcast #5

如果说大语言模型的跃迁，来自互联网沉淀下来的海量人类语言数据；那么在机器人世界里，是否也存在一种同样关键的数据？不是文字，不是图片，而是人拿起杯子、打开抽屉、穿过房间、和另一个人互动时，身体在物理世界里留下的痕迹。这个问题，正是我们这期节目最重要的关键词：human data，人类数据。

本期嘉宾 Danfei Xu:
Danfei 一直把自己定义成一个 roboticist，机器人学家，不是只在屏幕上训练模型的人，而是那个愿意坐在机器人旁边，看它动、看它坏、再把它修好的人。从早年折腾单片机小车，到斯坦福几乎从零搭建 robot learning 系统；从不被看好的 behavior cloning，到今天思考 human data 如何成为机器人学习的底层燃料，他的技术主线始终不是单点算法，而是一个完整全栈问题：机器人到底怎样才能从人类的动作、经验和互动中，学会进入真实世界？

在这期节目里，我们会聊徐丹飞如何走上机器人这条路，human data 会不会成为 robot learning 的 GPT moment的基石？人形机器人和人类数据究竟是谁成就谁？当机器人开始学习人的操作、人的身体、甚至人和人之间的互动，它的智能上限是什么？

这里是 WhynotTV Podcast。现在，请和我一起，走进Danfei Xu的世界。

---

00:02:31 -  Danfei Xu 最早如何对机器人产生兴趣
00:03:27 - 小时候的 Danfei Xu 是什么样的小孩
00:05:34 - 为什么高中时决定去美国读本科
00:06:38 - 一个人 DIY 申请美本的经历
00:08:44 - 美本申请受挫与选择 Dickinson College
00:11:40 - 18岁前的经历如何塑造了对不确定性的适应力
00:13:40 - 本科如何“打电话”进入 SynTouch 做机器人研究

items.summaryZh: 本期节目讨论了机器人学习中的“human data”（人类行为数据），即通过记录人类在真实物理世界中的动作、交互和触觉等多模态信息，作为机器人学习的基础燃料。嘉宾 Danfei Xu 解释了这种数据为何可能成为机器人领域类似 GPT‑3 的关键突破，并阐述了全栈机器人研发、行为克隆以及数据采集方式对行业的影响。
items.translationZh:
标题：Danfei Xu：人类数据，行为克隆，机器人GPT-3，全栈，EgoMimic，遥操作，UMI，斯坦福 | WhynotTV Podcast #5

正文：Danfei Xu：人类数据，行为克隆，机器人GPT-3，全栈，EgoMimic，遥操作，UMI，斯坦福 | WhynotTV Podcast #5

如果说大语言模型的跃迁，来自互联网沉淀下来的海量人类语言数据；那么在机器人世界里，是否也存在一种同样关键的数据？不是文字，不是图片，而是人拿起杯子、打开抽屉、穿过房间、和另一个人互动时，身体在物理世界里留下的痕迹。这个问题，正是我们这期节目最重要的关键词：human data，人类数据。

本期嘉宾 Danfei Xu:
Danfei 一直把自己定义成一个 roboticist，机器人学家，不是只在屏幕上训练模型的人，而是那个愿意坐在机器人旁边，看它动、看它坏、再把它修好的人。从早年折腾单片机小车，到斯坦福几乎从零搭建 robot learning 系统；从不被看好的 behavior cloning，到今天思考 human data 如何成为机器人学习的底层燃料，他的技术主线始终不是单点算法，而是一个完整全栈问题：机器人到底怎样才能从人类的动作、经验和互动中，学会进入真实世界？

在这期节目里，我们会聊徐丹飞如何走上机器人这条路，human data 会不会成为 robot learning 的 GPT moment的基石？人形机器人和人类数据究竟是谁成就谁？当机器人开始学习人的操作、人的身体、甚至人和人之间的互动，它的智能上限是什么？

这里是 WhynotTV Podcast。现在，请和我一起，走进Danfei Xu的世界。

---

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: 3D as code
items.textEn:
Text became the universal interface for software; 3D is becoming the universal interface for space. It’s the medium that allows humans and AI systems to generate, edit, simulate, and share worlds together.

There has never been a more exciting time for people and machines to work together. The interfaces connecting humans and machines are being redefined across the spectrum from abstract reasoning to physical tasks. Large language models spread quickly by building on text as a universal interface. Text is a shared medium, deeply embedded in our tools and habits; it can express ideas, give instructions, and communicate information between people and software. But what is the equivalent medium for describing and interacting with physical and virtual worlds?

We believe that interface is 3D, enabling communication, collaboration, and creation for people and machines alike. Three-dimensional representations allow people and machines to communicate about space, collaborate on designs, and create new environments. Over the past decades, a rich ecosystem of 3D tools and exchange formats has emerged to enable workflows that span ideation, modeling, simulation, presentation, and manufacturing.

This essay argues that there is an analogy between software code and 3D representations of the world. Building on the past decade of advances in neural graphics and simulation, a new generation of world models will accelerate existing spatial workflows and engender entirely new ones. Just as language models are revolutionizing software, world models will revolutionize how we shape and interact with virtual and physical worlds.

We can understand the role of 3D representations in the spatial realm by comparing them to code. Code is a durable abstraction built to specify the underlying logic executed by processors. For decades, it has powered much of the modern world. Today, AI models are becoming highly proficient at reasoning about and generating code; this code is then executed on hardware that predates LLMs by a large margin. As interfaces, code and 3D share important structural similarities in why and how we use them.

In both cases, the key property is externalization of state into structured artifacts that other systems can consume.

Consider an alternative approach in the ‘code’ domain. Instead of having an LLM write a program, we could ask it to be the program. For example, we could prompt an LLM: “Sort the following list of one million numbers.” The model is capable of attempting to simulate this behavior entirely within its token stream, by ingesting the list and trying to re-emit it in sorted order.

But we rarely use LLMs this way, except as a party trick, and would not expect them to perfectly succeed at such a task. Why? Because code execution provides guarantees that raw inference does not, such as reproducible execution, human readability, and modular composability. Code can be stored, versioned, tested, and run independently of the models’ transient context window. It separates reasoning, representation, and execution: you think about the algorithm, you write down a program as text, and then you run it.

There is a direct parallel in spatial systems. The equivalent of asking an LLM to “be the program” would be discarding structured world representations and simulation engines, and instead relying purely on a black-box entanglement of state and measurement, such as models for action-conditioned pixel or state generation queried frame-by-frame. Such models may excel at their core task and be useful for a variety of applications, but they lack manipulable structure: their outputs cannot be inspected, edited, easily shared (e.g., shared experienc…
items.summaryZh: 文章论述3D 正在成为描述和交互物理、虚拟空间的通用接口，类似文本对软件的作用，强调3D 表示能够让人类与 AI 协同生成、编辑、模拟和共享世界，对投资者而言意味着相关的3D 工具、平台和底层算力模型将迎来新一轮增长机会。
items.translationZh:
标题：3D as code

正文：文本已经成为软件的通用接口；3D 正在成为空间的通用接口。它是一种媒介，让人类和 AI 系统能够共同生成、编辑、模拟并共享世界。

从未有过如此激动人心的时刻，让人和机器一起协作。连接人类与机器的接口正从抽象推理到物理任务的全光谱范围内被重新定义。大型语言模型（LLM）通过基于文本这一通用接口快速传播。文本是一种共享的媒介，深植于我们的工具和习惯之中；它可以表达想法、给出指令、在人与软件之间传递信息。但是，描述和交互物理与虚拟世界的等价媒介是什么？

我们认为，这一接口就是 3D，它能够让人类和机器进行沟通、协作和创作。三维表示让人和机器能够就空间进行交流，协同设计，并创造新环境。过去几十年里，已经形成了一个丰富的 3D 工具和交换格式生态系统，以支持从构思、建模、仿真、展示到制造的工作流。

本文主张，3D 表示与软件代码之间存在类比。基于过去十年神经图形学和仿真技术的进步，新一代世界模型将加速现有空间工作流，并孕育全新工作流。正如语言模型正在革新软件，世界模型将革新我们塑造和交互虚拟及物理世界的方式。

我们可以通过将 3D 表示在空间领域的角色与代码进行比较来理解它们。代码是一种持久的抽象，用于指定处理器执行的底层逻辑。数十年来，它支撑了现代世界的多数技术。如今，AI 模型已经能够高度熟练地推理和生成代码；这些代码随后在远早于 LLM 的硬件上执行。作为接口，代码和 3D 在我们为何及如何使用它们方面具有重要的结构相似性。

在两者中，关键属性都是将状态外化为结构化的工件，以供其他系统消费。

考虑代码领域的另一种方式。与其让 LLM 编写程序，我们可以让它本身充当程序。例如，我们可以提示 LLM：“对以下一百万个数字的列表进行排序。”模型能够在其 token 流中尝试完整模拟此行为，读取列表并尝试重新输出已排序的列表。

但我们很少以这种方式使用 LLM，除非是作为一种技巧，并且也不会期望它们在此类任务上完美成功。原因是什么？因为代码执行提供了原始推理所不具备的保证，例如可重复执行、人类可读性以及模块化组合性。代码可以被存储、版本管理、测试，并在模型瞬时上下文窗口之外独立运行。它将推理、表示和执行分离：先思考算法，再把程序写成文本，最后运行它。

空间系统中存在直接的平行。让 LLM“成为程序”的等价做法是抛弃结构化的世界表示和仿真引擎，而仅依赖于一种状态与测量的黑盒纠缠，例如对动作条件化的像素或状态生成模型，逐帧查询。这类模型可能在其核心任务上表现卓越并对多种应用有用，但它们缺乏可操作的结构：其输出无法被检查、编辑，也难以轻松共享（例如共享经验…）

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: Announcing the World API
items.textEn:
A public API for generating explorable 3D worlds from text, images, and video — bringing Marble's world modeling capabilities into your applications.

Today, we’re launching the World API — a public interface for generating explorable 3D worlds using World Labs’ multimodal world model, Marble .

The World API makes it possible to create navigable spatial environments directly from text, images, panoramas, multi-view inputs, and video . These worlds can be rendered on the web, exported into downstream tools, or integrated into interactive systems and simulations.

This release marks an important step toward making spatial intelligence accessible wherever software is built .

The World API turns world creation into a programmable capability .

Instead of treating 3D environments as static assets or standalone tools, the API allows worlds to be generated on demand and embedded directly into products and workflows. Each request produces a complete 3D world that can be explored, shared, and built on.

This approach lowers the barrier to spatial creation and opens workflows that were previously slow, expensive, or out of reach.

The World API is designed to feel familiar to developers while remaining approachable to a broader audience.

At a high level, using the API means submitting a world generation request and receiving a fully navigable 3D environment in return. World generation runs asynchronously and works across a range of inputs, including text, images, and video.

There is no special setup and no proprietary tooling required. Worlds integrate naturally into modern applications, creative tools, and simulation pipelines.

Marble was created around a simple idea: World models should lift whatever signals you have into a full 3D understanding of space.

Each request returns a navigable 3D world that captures layout, depth, lighting, and spatial structure . These worlds can be rendered in the browser, exported into other tools, or used as the foundation for interactive experiences.

The World API is already being used across a range of domains. Below are three areas where world generation is changing how teams build products and experiences.

World generation allows teams to transform existing visual content into interactive spaces. Images, video, and cinematic sequences become explorable worlds that users step into rather than simply watch.

Platforms like Escape.ai are using the World API to turn 2D films into navigable 3D environments. A single video can generate both a spatial world and a viewing experience embedded within it — enabling new forms of interactive and social storytelling.

This shifts immersive media from passive playback toward environments that audiences can explore, share, and experience together.

In robotics and embodied AI, progress is limited by the lack of training data and plausible evaluation environments. Simulation plays a critical role — but building large numbers of diverse environments by hand is slow and costly.

The World API enables rapid generation of physically plausible environments from:

These environments can be integrated into modern simulators such as NVIDIA Isaac Sim , MuJoCo , and RoboSuite .

Research collaborations with teams like Lightwheel demonstrate how scalable world generation enables evaluation across many environments, rather than a small set of carefully reconstructed scenes.

Designers imagine space long before it is modeled. The World API allows early visual ideas to become explorable environments in seconds.

Tools like Interior AI bring spatial visualization to consumers, while platforms such as xFigura integrate world genera…
items.summaryZh: 该文宣布推出 World API，允许开发者通过文本、图像和视频等多模态输入即时生成可浏览的三维世界，降低空间内容创建门槛并可直接嵌入产品和仿真系统，对打造互动媒体、机器人仿真和设计可视化等领域具有重要意义。
items.translationZh:
标题：Announcing the World API

正文：一个公共 API，可从文本、图像和视频生成可探索的 3D 世界——将 Marble 的世界建模能力引入您的应用程序。

今天，我们推出了 World API——一个使用 World Labs 的多模态世界模型 Marble 的公共接口，用于生成可探索的 3D 世界。

World API 使得能够直接从文本、图像、全景图、多视角输入和视频创建可导航的空间环境。这些世界可以在网页上渲染、导出到下游工具，或集成到交互系统和仿真中。

此发布标志着将空间智能带到任何软件构建场所的重要一步。

World API 将世界创建转变为可编程的能力。

它不再把 3D 环境视为静态资产或独立工具，API 允许按需生成世界并直接嵌入产品和工作流。每个请求都会生成一个完整的 3D 世界，可供探索、分享和进一步构建。

这种方式降低了空间创作的门槛，打开了以前慢速、昂贵或难以实现的工作流程。

World API 旨在让开发者感到熟悉，同时对更广泛的受众保持亲和。

从高层次来看，使用该 API 意味着提交一次世界生成请求，并返回一个完全可导航的 3D 环境。世界生成是异步进行的，支持文本、图像和视频等多种输入。

无需特殊设置，也不需要专有工具。生成的世界可以自然地集成到现代应用、创意工具和仿真管道中。

Marble 的创建基于一个简单的理念：世界模型应当把您拥有的任何信号提升为对空间的完整 3D 理解。

每个请求返回一个可导航的 3D 世界，捕获布局、深度、光照和空间结构。这些世界可以在浏览器中渲染、导出到其他工具，或作为交互体验的基础。

World API 已在多个领域得到应用。以下是三个世界生成正在改变团队构建产品和体验的方向。

世界生成使团队能够将现有视觉内容转化为交互空间。图像、视频和电影序列不再仅供观看，而是变成用户可以步入的可探索世界。

像 Escape.ai 这样的平台正在使用 World API 将二维影片转化为可导航的 3D 环境。单个视频即可生成空间世界和嵌入其中的观看体验——实现新的交互式和社交叙事形式。

这将沉浸式媒体从被动播放转向观众可以共同探索、分享和体验的环境。

在机器人与具身 AI 领域，进展受限于缺乏训练数据和可信的评估环境。仿真发挥关键作用——但手工构建大量多样化环境既慢又昂贵。

World API 能快速从以下来源生成物理合理的环境：

这些环境可集成到诸如 NVIDIA Isaac Sim、MuJoCo 和 RoboSuite 等现代仿真器中。

与 Lightwheel 等团队的研究合作表明，可扩展的世界生成使得能够在众多环境上进行评估，而不是仅限于少量精心重建的场景。

设计师在建模之前就会构想空间。World API 让早期的视觉概念在秒级内转化为可探索的环境。

类似 Interior AI 的工具将空间可视化带给消费者，而像 xFigura 这样的平台则将世界生成整合进其产品中。

items.author: https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UC5xLV_gJAP9psKcyrJ3ZIcw
items.title: 翁家翌：OpenAI，GPT，强化学习，Infra，后训练，天授，tuixue，开源，CMU，清华｜WhynotTV Podcast #4
items.textEn:
翁家翌：OpenAI，GPT，强化学习，Infra，后训练，天授，tuixue，开源，CMU，清华｜WhynotTV Podcast #4

本期嘉宾翁家翌。他在 2022 年加入 OpenAI，并且是 OpenAI 一系列核心模型背后的核心贡献者之一——从 GPT-3.5、GPT-4、再到 GPT-5，你能看到的那些关键跃迁里，都有他的身影；而他最主要的贡献，你可以先记住三个词：强化学习、post-training、infra。 

但对我来说，翁家翌不只是“把模型做得更强的人”。在成为 OpenAI 研究员之前，他就已经用开源和产品影响过无数人：把知识与资料公开、试图打破信息差；把做工具称作一种“慈善”——在他的价值观里，开源不是履历装饰，而是一种对世界的投入方式：追求的不是掌声，而是 impact。 

在这期节目里，我们会从翁家翌的童年经历聊起，走到他在清华与 CMU 的求学与成长，再到他在 2022 年加入 OpenAI 后的亲历：站在 AI 风暴中心的人，到底看见了什么？ 这里是 WhynotTV Podcast。现在请和我一起，走进翁家翌的世界。

items.translationZh:
标题：翁家翌：OpenAI，GPT，强化学习，Infra，后训练，天授，tuixue，开源，CMU，清华｜WhynotTV Podcast #4

正文：翁家翌：Open​AI，GPT，强化学习，Infra，后训练，天授，tuixue，开源，CMU，清华｜WhynotTV Podcast #4

本期嘉宾翁家翌。他在 2022 年加入 Open​AI，并且是 Open​AI 一系列核心模型背后的核心贡献者之一——从 GPT‑3.5、GPT‑4、再到 GPT‑5，你能看到的那些关键跃迁里，都有他的身影；而他最主要的贡献，你可以先记住三个词：强化学习（RL），后训练（post‑training），基础设施（infra）。

但对我来说，翁家翌不只是“把模型做得更强的人”。在成为 Open​AI 研究员之前，他就已经用开源和产品影响过无数人：把知识与资料公开、试图打破信息差；把做工具称作一种“慈善”——在他的价值观里，开源不是履历装饰，而是一种对世界的投入方式：追求的不是掌声，而是 impact（影响）。

在这期节目里，我们会从翁家翌的童年经历聊起，走到他在清华与 CMU 的求学与成长，再到他在 2022 年加入 Open​AI 后的亲历：站在 AI 风暴中心的人，到底看见了什么？ 这里是 WhynotTV Podcast。现在请和我一起，走进翁家翌的世界。

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: Marble: A Multimodal World Model
items.textEn:
Marble, our frontier multimodal world model, is available to everyone starting today

Spatial intelligence is the next frontier in AI, demanding powerful world models to realize its full potential. World models should reconstruct, generate, and simulate 3D worlds; and allow both humans and agents to interact with them. Spatially intelligent world models will transform a wide variety of industries over the coming years.

Two months ago we shared a preview of Marble , our World Model that creates 3D worlds from image or text prompts. Since then, Marble has been available to an early set of beta users to create 3D worlds for themselves.

Today we are making Marble, a first-in-class generative multimodal world model, generally available for anyone to use. We have also drastically expanded Marble's capabilities, and are excited to highlight them here:

Multimodal Marble : Marble is now massively multimodal. Marble can create 3D worlds from text, images, video, or coarse 3D layouts; Marble also lets you interactively edit, expand, and combine worlds. Once generated, 3D worlds can be exported as Gaussian splats, meshes, or videos. These new capabilities let users create and edit worlds with fine-grained control; and makes those worlds more useful than ever before.

Marble Labs : We are launching Marble Labs , a creative hub where imagination meets experimentation. It is where artists, engineers, and designers push the boundaries of world models, showcasing bold ideas, real-world workflows, and new possibilities across gaming, VFX, design, robotics, and beyond. Marble Labs is also home to in-depth case studies, tutorials, and documentation that give anyone the tools to learn, build, and share their own 3D worlds.

Sign up at marble.worldlabs.ai and start creating worlds for yourself!

Our human experience of the world is inherently multimodal: we use all of our senses to make sense of the world around us. We integrate sight, sound, touch, and language to build up a mental model of the outside world; these different representations work together, enriching and reinforcing each other to let us reason about the world and act within it.

World models should work similarly. They should be massively multimodal, able to lift whatever input signals are available into a full 3D world, and they should be able to iteratively update their understanding of the world as new information becomes available.

Marble is the first of its kind - a next-generation world model making strides toward this vision. It can now create 3D worlds from a wide variety of input types, and lets users iteratively edit or expand worlds.

Marble's new capabilities let you dive as deep as you want in controlling your generated worlds. You can quickly create full 3D worlds from a simple image or text prompt or interactively edit worlds in both 2D and 3D, bringing to life a precise vision of a world in your mind.

To start, Marble can create a full 3D world from a single image or a short text prompt. This is the simplest and easiest way to create worlds. Marble can generate worlds with a wide variety of scene types and artistic styles.

Image prompts make it easy to combine Marble with other AI tools. You can generate images with your favorite image generation model, then bring it to Marble to lift it to a full 3D world.

Text and image prompts are intuitive and powerful, but limited in creative control: Marble must invent all the details of the world that are not present in the input text or image prompt. This is often magical; but sometimes you may want to …
items.summaryZh: Marble 推出面向大众的多模态世界模型，能够从文本、图片、视频等输入生成并编辑 3D 世界，并提供导出和实验平台，这对游戏、VFX、机器人等行业的创新和应用具有重要意义。
items.translationZh:
标题：Marble：一种多模态世界模型

正文：Marble，我们的前沿多模态世界模型，今天起向所有人开放。

空间智能是 AI 的下一个前沿领域，需要强大的世界模型才能实现其全部潜力。世界模型应当能够重建、生成和模拟 3D 世界，并让人类和代理与之交互。空间智能的世界模型将在未来几年改造各种行业。

两个月前我们预览了 Marble——一个能够根据图像或文本提示创建 3D 世界的世界模型。此后，Marble 已向一批早期 beta 用户开放，供其自行创建 3D 世界。

今天我们将 Marble 这一一流的生成式多模态世界模型向所有人全面开放。我们还大幅扩展了 Marble 的功能，以下是重点展示：

多模态 Marble：Marble 现在实现了大规模多模态。Marble 可以根据文本、图片、视频或粗略的 3D 布局创建 3D 世界；它还支持交互式编辑、扩展和合并世界。生成后，3D 世界可导出为 Gaussian splats（高斯点云）、网格（meshes）或视频。这些新功能让用户能够细粒度控制并使世界更具实用性。

Marble Labs：我们推出了 Marble Labs，一个创意中心，让想象力与实验相结合。在这里，艺术家、工程师和设计师推动世界模型的边界，展示游戏、VFX、设计、机器人等领域的大胆想法、真实工作流和新可能性。Marble Labs 还提供深入案例研究、教程和文档，为任何人提供学习、构建和分享自己 3D 世界的工具。

请访问 marble.worldlabs.ai 注册并开始为自己创建世界！

我们的世界经验本质上是多模态的：我们使用所有感官来理解周围的世界。我们融合视觉、听觉、触觉和语言，构建对外部世界的心理模型；这些不同的表征相互协作、相互强化，使我们能够推理并在世界中行动。

世界模型应当同样工作。它们应当是大规模多模态的，能够将任何可用的输入信号提升为完整的 3D 世界，并且能够在新信息出现时迭代更新对世界的理解。

Marble 是首个此类模型——一种迈向此愿景的下一代世界模型。它现在可以从多种输入类型创建 3D 世界，并允许用户迭代编辑或扩展世界。

Marble 的新功能让你可以在生成的世界中深度控制。你可以通过简单的图像或文本提示快速创建完整的 3D 世界，或在 2D 与 3D 中交互式编辑世界，将脑海中的精准愿景付诸现实。

首先，Marble 可以从单张图像或简短文本提示创建完整的 3D 世界。这是创建世界最简单、最容易的方式。Marble 能生成各种场景类型和艺术风格的世界。

图像提示使 Marble 与其他 AI 工具的结合更为便捷。你可以使用喜欢的图像生成模型生成图片，然后将其导入 Marble，提升为完整的 3D 世界。

文本和图像提示直观且强大，但在创意控制方面有限：Marble 必须自行想象输入文本或图像中未出现的细节。这常常是神奇的；但有时你可能希望…

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: RTFM: A Real-Time Frame Model
items.textEn:
A research preview of RTFM, a new generative world model that generates video in real-time as you interact with it.

Today we’re sharing RTFM, a new real-time generative World Model. RTFM (Real-Time Frame Model) generates video in real-time as you interact with it. It can be used to explore generated 3D worlds and real-world locations. RTFM is available today as a research preview.

World Models are Compute Hungry We are excited for a future where powerful World Models can reconstruct, generate, and simulate persistent, interactive, and physically accurate worlds in real‑time. Such models will transform industries from media to robotics and beyond. The past year has been an exciting time for this emerging technology, as advances in generative video modeling have been applied to generative world modeling.

As this technology develops, one thing is becoming clear: generative World Models will be very computationally demanding, much more so than LLMs today. If we naively apply modern video architectures to this problem, generating an interactive 4K video stream at 60fps requires producing over 100K tokens per second (roughly the length of Frankenstein or the first Harry Potter book); keeping these generations persistent for an hour or more of interaction requires attending to contexts of well over 100M tokens. This is neither feasible nor economically viable given today’s computing infrastructure.

We are strong believers in The Bitter Lesson : simple methods that scale gracefully with increasing compute tend to dominate in AI, since they can benefit from the exponentially decreasing costs of compute that have driven all of technology forward for decades. Generative World Models are perfectly positioned to benefit from a future where the cost of computation continues to fall.

This leads to a natural question: are generative World Models blocked by today’s hardware constraints? Or are there ways to preview this technology today?

We set forth with a simple goal: Design a generative World Model that is efficient enough to deploy today, that can continue to scale up with more compute. Our ambitious target was building a model that can be deployed on a single H100 GPU, maintaining both interactive framerates and worlds that persist no matter how long you interact with them. Achieving these constraints would let us pull the future forward, delivering an experience today that gives a hint of what these models could achieve in the future.

This goal influenced the design of our entire system, from task setup to model architecture. We carefully optimized all parts of our inference stack, applying the latest advances in architecture design, model distillation, and inference optimization in order to give the highest-fidelity preview of tomorrow’s models running on today’s hardware.

Traditional 3D graphics pipelines model the world using an explicit 3D representation (e.g. triangle mesh, Gaussian splats) which is then rendered to produce 2D images. They use hand‑designed data structures and algorithms to model 3D geometry, materials, lighting, shadows, reflections, and more. These approaches have been the trusted workhorse of computer graphics for decades, but they do not trivially scale to more data and compute.

RTFM takes a different approach. It builds on recent advances in generative video modeling and trains a single neural network that inputs one or more 2D images of a scene, and generates 2D images of that scene from new viewpoints without building any explicit 3D representation of the world. RTFM is implemented as an autoregressive diffusion transformer …
items.summaryZh: 本文介绍了实时生成式世界模型RTFM（Real-Time Frame Model），它能够在用户交互时实时生成视频，展示了在单卡H100 GPU上实现交互帧率和持久世界的可能性，对未来媒体、机器人等行业的技术路径具有指示意义。
items.translationZh:
标题：RTFM：实时帧模型

正文：RTFM的研究预览，一种能够在交互过程中实时生成视频的新型生成式世界模型。

今天我们分享RTFM，这个全新的实时生成式世界模型。RTFM（Real-Time Frame Model）在你与之交互时实时生成视频。它可用于探索生成的3D世界和真实世界地点。RTFM现已作为研究预览提供。

世界模型对计算资源需求极高 我们对未来充满期待——强大的世界模型能够实时重建、生成和模拟持久、交互式且物理精准的世界。这类模型将改变从媒体到机器人等各行各业。过去一年，生成式视频建模的进展已被应用于生成式世界模型，这是一个令人兴奋的新兴技术。

随着技术的发展，有一点越来越明确：生成式世界模型的计算需求将远超当前的LLM（大语言模型）。如果我们仅仅把现代视频架构套用到该问题上，生成一个交互式4K、60帧/秒的视频流需要每秒产生超过10万 tokens（大致相当于《弗兰肯斯坦》或《哈利·波特》第一本书的长度）；若要让这些生成在交互超过一小时后仍保持持久，则需要处理超过1亿 tokens 的上下文。这在当今的计算基础设施下既不可行，也在经济上不可接受。

我们坚信 “苦涩教训”（The Bitter Lesson）：能够随着计算资源的提升而平滑扩展的简易方法往往在AI领域占据主导，因为它们能够受益于过去数十年推动技术进步的计算成本指数级下降。生成式世界模型正好处于能够受益于未来计算成本继续下降的有利位置。

这引出了一个自然的问题：生成式世界模型是否受限于当今的硬件约束？亦或是有办法在今天预览这项技术？

我们的目标很简单：设计一种足够高效、能够今天部署的生成式世界模型，并且能够随着计算资源的增加继续扩展。我们的雄心目标是构建一个能够在单块 H100 GPU 上部署的模型，同时保持交互式帧率并且世界能够在任意长时间的交互后仍保持持久。实现这些约束将让我们把未来提前到今天，提供一种体验，让人们一窥这些模型未来可能达到的水平。

这个目标影响了我们整个系统的设计，从任务设置到模型架构。我们对推理栈的各个环节进行仔细优化，采用了最新的架构设计、模型蒸馏和推理优化技术，以在当今硬件上运行明日模型的最高保真预览。

传统的3D图形管线使用显式的3D表示（例如三角网格、Gaussian splats）来建模世界，然后渲染生成2D图像。它们使用手工设计的数据结构和算法来建模3D几何、材质、光照、阴影、反射等。这些方法数十年来一直是计算机图形学的可靠支柱，但它们并不能轻易地随数据和计算的增加而扩展。

RTFM采取了不同的方式。它基于近期生成式视频建模的进展，训练一个单一神经网络，该网络以场景的一张或多张2D图像为输入，并从新的视角生成该场景的2D图像，而无需构建任何显式的3D世界表示。RTFM实现为自回归扩散 Transformer …

items.author: Danijar Hafner
items.authorName: Danijar Hafner · 世界模型 / Dreamer
items.title: Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models
items.textEn:
Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models

World models learn general knowledge from videos and simulate experience for training behaviors in imagination, offering a path towards intelligent agents. However, previous world models have been unable to accurately predict object interactions in complex environments. We introduce Dreamer 4, a scalable agent that learns to solve control tasks by reinforcement learning inside of a fast and accurate world model. In the complex video game Minecraft, the world model accurately predicts object interactions and game mechanics, outperforming previous world models by a large margin. The world model achieves real-time interactive inference on a single GPU through a shortcut forcing objective and an efficient transformer architecture. Moreover, the world model learns general action conditioning from only a small amount of data, allowing it to extract the majority of its knowledge from diverse unlabeled videos. We propose the challenge of obtaining diamonds in Minecraft from only offline data, aligning with practical applications such as robotics where learning from environment interaction can be unsafe and slow. This task requires choosing sequences of over 20,000 mouse and keyboard actions from raw pixels. By learning behaviors in imagination, Dreamer 4 is the first agent to obtain diamonds in Minecraft purely from offline data, without environment interaction. Our work provides a scalable recipe for imagination training, marking a step towards intelligent agents.
items.summaryZh: 本文介绍了 Dreamer 4，一种能够在高速且精准的世界模型中通过强化学习训练的可扩展智能体。它在《我的世界》里实现了对复杂对象交互的准确预测，并首次仅凭离线视频数据在想象中完成获取钻石的任务，展示了对机器人等实际场景的潜在价值。
items.translationZh:
Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models

World models learn general knowledge from videos and simulate experience for training behaviors in imagination, offering a path towards intelligent agents. However, previous world models have been unable to accurately predict object interactions in complex environments. We introduce Dreamer 4, a scalable agent that learns to solve control tasks by reinforcement learning inside of a fast and accurate world model. In the complex video game Minecraft, the world model accurately predicts object interactions and game mechanics, outperforming previous world models by a large margin. The world model achieves real-time interactive inference on a single GPU through a shortcut forcing objective and an efficient transformer architecture. Moreover, the world model learns general action conditioning from only a small amount of data, allowing it to extract the majority of its knowledge from diverse unlabeled videos. We propose the challenge of obtaining diamonds in Minecraft from only offline data, aligning with practical applications such as robotics where learning from environment interaction can be unsafe and slow. This task requires choosing sequences of over 20,000 mouse and keyboard actions from raw pixels. By learning behaviors in imagination, Dreamer 4 is the first agent to obtain diamonds in Minecraft purely from offline data, without environment interaction. Our work provides a scalable recipe for imagination training, marking a step towards intelligent agents.

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: Generating Bigger and Better Worlds
items.textEn:
Announcing our latest breakthrough in 3D world generation with larger, more detailed environments and improved fidelity.

Today we're sharing an update on our quest to push the frontier of spatial intelligence and generate persistent, navigable, and controllable 3D worlds. We are also introducing Marble, a limited access beta preview of our model today at marble.worldlabs.ai , where users can view and create 3D worlds.

Given an image or text prompt, our model generates a 3D world that you can explore for as long as you wish — no time limits, no morphing, no inconsistency. Compared to our previous results , our generated worlds are bigger, more stylistically diverse, and have cleaner 3D geometry.

Marble makes our model available for users to start viewing and building worlds today. Enthusiasts and builders can export generated worlds as Gaussian splats and use them in downstream projects. This is particularly easy to do with our open‑source rendering library Spark which seamlessly integrates Gaussian splats into Three.js for building web‑based 3D experiences, and renders efficiently on desktops, laptops, mobile devices, and VR headsets.

Our model's consistency and style adherence now make it possible to build out large worlds by composing individual generations, as shown in the banner video above and the examples at the end of this post.

World Labs provides a solution by creating a splat navigable 3D stage so that I can plan a shoot in a coherent and consistent 3D set like a filmmaker.

I created a world with two different photos from a trip, and then one of your pre-loaded images. Fun to zoom in and out, discover unexpected underbelly of the images.

The ability to navigate and interact with a consistent 3D world lies at the heart of many workflows and use cases for visual creators. Our generated 3D worlds allow for free viewpoint navigation, in the user's browser, at zero cost. In contrast to the limited 3D experience provided by depth maps or point clouds, our worlds have richer geometric complexity and produce more complete 3D worlds, allowing users to look behind and beyond what is visible in the input view.

Our current model is designed to create 3D environments rather than focusing on isolated or central objects, such as people or animals. Sorry to disappoint selfie enthusiasts or loving pet owners — for now.

Explore in Marble More diverse styles What impresses me most about World Labs is its versatility. Being able to generate hyper-realistic environments just as easily as stylized cartoon or anime worlds opens up an incredible range of creative possibilities.

One of the most exciting aspects of working with generative AI is the ability to freely iterate over look and feel as part of the creative process. Our model can lift inputs of many varying styles into 3D, from flat and colorful cartoons to realistic and richly detailed imagery, allowing for exploration and iteration to find just the right world for the job.

Getting persistent 3D geometry at scale is a really really hard problem. Having a model that just focuses on that will be central to a lot of workflows (anything needing composition, stitching, large memory, ability to edit / stack persistently etc.)

Room-sized worlds are a great start, but creators' imagination often extends beyond these boundaries. We want to share a glimpse of what's possible as users compose their generated spaces together to build out much larger environments. Given the stylistic coherence and geometric consistency achieved by our model, it's already fun to be able to navigate, b…
items.summaryZh: World Labs推出了Marble平台的全新3D世界生成模型，可根据图像或文字提示生成更大、更细致且风格统一的可持续导航3D场景，对内容创作者和视觉工作流具有重要意义。

items.author: https://www.youtube.com/feeds/videos.xml?channel_id=UC5xLV_gJAP9psKcyrJ3ZIcw
items.title: 陈天奇：机器学习系统，长期主义，初心，XGBoost，MXNet，TVM，MLC LLM，OctoML，CMU，UW，ACM班
items.textEn:
陈天奇：机器学习系统，长期主义，初心，XGBoost，MXNet，TVM，MLC LLM，OctoML，CMU，UW，ACM班

当我问陈天奇，你会对十年前二十年前的陈天奇说些什么时，他沉默了近20秒说——

“可能要反过来，我需要过去的我对现在的我，现在的我对未来的我说，不要忘记自己的初心，坚持理想”



那一刻我被真正感动了——因为我真正看到一个真正的理想主义者，过去二十年机器学习的历史， XGBoost、MXNet、TVM、MLC都是不可磨灭的精彩印迹，而在这几个项目名背后，有一个共同的名字，他就是本期播客嘉宾陈天奇。过去 15 年，陈天奇把让机器学习模型更轻、更快、更易部署做成了一条清晰的技术主线。从最早的把树模型系统推到极致的XGBoost，到最早的深度学习框架之一 MXNet，再到开创深度学习编译领域的TVM，和今天希望把大模型跑上所有设备的MLC LLM。



陈天奇在机器学习系统这条路上已经走了快 20 年——从陈天奇的视角看这 20 年的激荡会是什么样的风景？在这期播客，你会听到：

我和陈天奇会把他的每一个开源项目都拆解清楚——他们为什么诞生、如何长成、在哪里拐弯。
也会走进陈天奇的个人经历——年少如何与计算机相遇，高二的时候如何自学写编译器，在交大 ACM 班打下系统功底，在 UW 的科研突破，到最后同时走向创业和教职两条路。
更重要的我们还聊到了陈天奇的底层价值观——长期主义如何落地，如何在不确定性中坚持敢失败的勇气和初心。在科研、创业、教职三条路都获得成功后，陈天奇在筹划着什么样的下一步？


这里是WhynotTV Podcast——现在，请和我一起进入陈天奇的世界。

items.summaryZh: 节目回顾了机器学习系统领军人物陈天奇二十年的技术与人生轨迹，解析他从 XGBoost、MXNet、TVM 到 MLC LLM 等开源项目的诞生与演进，以及他坚持长期主义、初心和敢于失败的价值观，这对关注 AI 基础设施和创新生态的投资者了解行业趋势与关键人才具有参考价值。
items.translationZh:
标题：陈天奇：机器学习系统，长期主义，初心，XGBoost，MXNet，TVM，MLC LLM，OctoML，CMU，UW，ACM班

正文：陈天奇：机器学习系统，长期主义，初心，XGBoost，MXNet，TVM，MLC LLM，OctoML，CMU，UW，ACM班

当我问陈天奇，你会对十年前二十年前的陈天奇说些什么时，他沉默了近20秒说——

“可能要反过来，我需要过去的我对现在的我，现在的我对未来的我说，不要忘记自己的初心，坚持理想”

那一刻我被真正感动了——因为我真正看到一个真正的理想主义者，过去二十年机器学习的历史， XGBoost、MXNet、TVM、MLC都是不可磨灭的精彩印迹，而在这几个项目名背后，有一个共同的名字，他就是本期播客嘉宾陈天奇。过去 15 年，陈天奇把让机器学习模型更轻、更快、更易部署做成了一条清晰的技术主线。从最早的把树模型系统推到极致的XGBoost，到最早的深度学习框架之一 MXNet，再到开创深度学习编译领域的TVM，和今天希望把大模型跑上所有设备的MLC LLM。

陈天奇在机器学习系统这条路上已经走了快 20 年——从陈天奇的视角看这 20 年的激荡会是什么样的风景？在这期播客，你会听到：

我和陈天奇会把他的每一个开源项目都拆解清楚——他们为什么诞生、如何长成、在哪里拐弯。
也会走进陈天奇的个人经历——年少如何与计算机相遇，高二的时候如何自学写编译器，在交大 ACM 班打下系统功底，在 UW 的科研突破，到最后同时走向创业和教职两条路。
更重要的我们还聊到了陈天奇的底层价值观——长期主义如何落地，如何在不确定性中坚持敢失败的勇气和初心。在科研、创业、教职三条路都获得成功后，陈天奇在筹划着什么样的下一步？

这里是WhynotTV Podcast——现在，请和我一起进入陈天奇的世界。

items.author: wm-dreamer4-2025
items.title: Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models（Dreamer 4）
items.textEn:
Venue: 2025
Authors: Hafner et al.
Training Agents Inside of Scalable World Models (Dreamer 4): scales world models to larger, more complex environments toward a 'foundation model for control', letting agents learn complex tasks almost entirely inside an imagined world. The latest step in scaling up world models.
items.summaryZh: 可扩展世界模型中的智能体训练（Dreamer 4）：把世界模型扩展到更大规模、更复杂的环境，朝「面向控制的基础模型」迈进，让智能体几乎完全在想象出的世界里学习复杂任务。世界模型走向规模化的最新一步。

items.title: 胡渊鸣：Meshy AI，太极，MIT，清华姚班，图形学，物理仿真模拟，开源，商业化，勇气 ，智慧 ｜ WhynotTV Podcast #2
items.textEn:
胡渊鸣：Meshy AI，太极，MIT，清华姚班，图形学，物理仿真模拟，开源，商业化，勇气 ，智慧 ｜ WhynotTV Podcast #2

今年四月我读了一篇非常打动我的Meshy AI CEO胡渊鸣写的知乎文章《当CEO重读PhD：论勇气与智慧》，当时的我就在憧憬要是能和胡渊鸣畅谈一个下午会是多么难忘的体验……

没想到四个月后真的实现了！我非常非常非常享受和胡渊鸣做这期播客，3小时40分钟的素材，我剪辑过程中反复把素材过了很多遍，每一遍听都有更新和更深的收获，我很激动也很感谢渊鸣——我们录制出了一期这么精彩的内容！

我们以渊鸣的的人生经历为引子——童年时期开始写物理模拟器和游戏，清华姚班和MIT的求学经历，太极项目的前世今生和太极2.0，Meshy的艰难商业转型到现在用户超400万，年营收增长超10倍，所有经历本质都是在反射渊鸣对科技、商业、世界、人生的本质思考，强烈推荐！

千言万语一句话——
这个世界需要更多胡渊鸣！

------------------------------------------------------------------

items.title: 杨硕：妙动科技，特斯拉Optimus，CMU大疆，无人机，人形机器人 ｜ WhynotTV Podcast #1
items.textEn:
杨硕：妙动科技，特斯拉Optimus，CMU大疆，无人机，人形机器人 ｜ WhynotTV Podcast #1

以下内容是我和杨硕的对话。

杨硕本科和硕士毕业于香港科技大学，在港科大以及毕业后的5年期间，杨硕参与并主导了了大疆飞控系统的研发。

大疆之后，杨硕于2018年前往CMU攻读机器人博士，专注足式机器人的状态估计与控制算法，

杨硕于2023年博士毕业后加入了特斯拉Optimus人形机器人团队，一年半，杨硕离职特斯拉，宣布创立妙动科技，目标设计研发消费级机器人产品。

过去十年，他在知乎的账号分享了大量机器人技术见解与一线科研生活，至今仍鼓励着今天年轻一辈的机器人学生继续前进。

Venue: arXiv · Saturday Robotics Club 13

items.textEn:
Venue: arXiv · Saturday Robotics Club 17
Reading Club 17 selection — ManiFeel: Benchmarking and Understanding Visuotactile Manipulation Policy Learning.
items.summaryZh: Club 17「软触觉为中心的多模态智能」选读。ManiFeel：对视觉-触觉（visuotactile）操作策略学习进行基准测试与理解。

items.author: Stanford
items.title: CS336：从零构建语言模型（Stanford · 2025 春）
items.textEn:
Type: course
Stanford CS336 'Language Modeling from Scratch' (Spring 2025) course homepage: from data, tokenization, Transformer architecture, training and parallelism to alignment, building a language model from scratch. Lectures, assignments and readings are public. One of the best systematic courses for actually understanding what happens inside an LLM rather than staying at the concept level.
items.summaryZh: 斯坦福 CS336《Language Modeling from Scratch》2025 春季课程主页：从数据、分词、Transformer 架构、训练、并行到对齐，带学生从零亲手实现一个语言模型。讲义、作业与阅读全部公开。想真正搞懂 LLM「里面到底发生了什么」、不止停留在概念层的最佳系统性课程之一。

items.author: sr12-autoeval-2503
items.title: AutoEval：机器人策略的自动评测
items.textEn:
Venue: arXiv · Saturday Robotics Club 12
Reading Club 12 selection — AutoEval (autonomous evaluation methodology for robot policies).
items.summaryZh: Club 12 选读。AutoEval：机器人策略评测方法学，朝自动化、可复现的评测推进。

items.author: sr10-toddlerbot-2502

items.textEn:
Venue: arXiv · Saturday Robotics Club 10
Reading Club 10 selection — ToddlerBot: Open-Source ML-Compatible Humanoid Platform for Loco-Manipulation.
items.summaryZh: Club 10 选读。ToddlerBot：面向 ML 的开源人形机器人平台，用于运动-操作（loco-manipulation）研究。

items.author: https://www.worldlabs.ai/blog
items.authorName: World Labs · 李飞飞（空间智能 / 世界模型）
items.title: Generating Worlds
items.textEn:
Sharing our early progress toward persistent, navigable 3D worlds you can explore in your browser.

Renderers, Simulators, Planners, and the Loop That Connects Them

A technical deep dive into Spark 2.0's streamable, Level-of-Detail system for 3D Gaussian Splatting.
items.summaryZh: 该文介绍了公司在浏览器中实现可持续、可导航的3D世界的早期进展，重点说明了 Spark 2.0 的可流式、细节层次（Level‑of‑Detail）系统如何支持3D 高斯点渲染，以及其在渲染器、模拟器、规划器之间的闭环技术实现，对关注前沿交互式3D技术的投资者具有参考价值。
items.translationZh:
标题：Generating Worlds

正文：分享我们在浏览器中实现可持续、可导航的 3D 世界的早期进展。

渲染器、模拟器、规划器，以及连接它们的闭环

对 Spark 2.0 的流式、细节层次（Level‑of‑Detail）系统进行技术深度剖析，该系统用于 3D 高斯点（Gaussian Splatting）渲染。

items.author: Danijar Hafner
items.authorName: Danijar Hafner · 世界模型 / Dreamer
items.title: One Step Diffusion via Shortcut Models
items.textEn:
One Step Diffusion via Shortcut Models

Diffusion models and flow-matching models have enabled generating diverse and realistic images by learning to transfer noise to data. However, sampling from these models involves iterative denoising over many neural network passes, making generation slow and expensive. Previous approaches for speeding up sampling require complex training regimes, such as multiple training phases, multiple networks, or fragile scheduling. We introduce shortcut models, a family of generative models that use a single network and training phase to produce high-quality samples in a single or multiple sampling steps. Shortcut models condition the network not only on the current noise level but also on the desired step size, allowing the model to skip ahead in the generation process. Across a wide range of sampling step budgets, shortcut models consistently produce higher quality samples than previous approaches, such as consistency models and reflow. Compared to distillation, shortcut models reduce complexity to a single network and training phase and additionally allow varying step budgets at inference time.
items.summaryZh: 文章介绍了一种名为shortcut模型的生成模型，仅用单一网络和一次训练即可在单步或多步采样中生成高质量图像，解决了扩散模型采样慢、成本高的问题。这对投资者而言重要，因为该技术有望显著降低生成式AI的算力成本，提高商业化效率。
items.translationZh:
One Step Diffusion via Shortcut Models

扩散模型（Diffusion models）和流匹配模型（flow-matching models）通过学习将噪声转化为数据，实现了多样且逼真的图像生成。然而，这些模型的采样过程需要在多个神经网络迭代中进行去噪，使得生成速度慢且成本高。此前加速采样的方法往往需要复杂的训练体系，例如多阶段训练、多网络组合或脆弱的调度策略。

我们提出了shortcut模型，这是一类生成模型，只使用单一网络和一次训练，即可在单步或多步采样中产生高质量样本。shortcut模型不仅让网络在当前噪声水平上进行条件化，还让其根据所需的步长进行条件化，从而使模型能够在生成过程中跨越若干步骤直接前进。

在广泛的采样步数预算范围内，shortcut模型始终比之前的方法（如一致性模型（consistency models）和reflow）产生更高质量的样本。相较于蒸馏（distillation）技术，shortcut模型将复杂度降低到单一网络和一次训练，并且在推理时还能灵活调整步数预算。

items.author: sr08-egomimic-2410
items.title: EgoMimic：从第一人称人类视频学习灵巧操作
items.textEn:
Venue: arXiv · Saturday Robotics Club 08/13
Reading Club 08 selection — Learning Dexterous Manipulation from Egocentric Human Videos (a.k.a. EgoMimic): using first-person human video as scalable data for robot manipulation policies.
items.summaryZh: Saturday Robotics 读书会 Club 08「具身人类数据：动作与行为的互联网」选读。研究如何把第一人称（egocentric）人类视频作为大规模数据，迁移到机器人灵巧操作策略学习。具身智能「用人类数据训机器人」路线的代表工作。

items.author: wm-dynalang-2023
items.title: Dynalang：用语言预测未来的多模态世界模型
items.textEn:
Venue: ICML 2024
Authors: Lin et al.
Dynalang: an agent learns a multimodal world model by predicting future text and images, so it can genuinely 'understand the world through language' and act on it. By folding language into the world model's prediction targets, it bridges large language models and embodied control.
items.summaryZh: Dynalang：让智能体通过预测未来的文本与图像来学习一个多模态世界模型，从而真正「用语言理解世界」并据此行动。把语言纳入世界模型预测目标，连接了大模型与具身控制两条线。

items.author: Danijar Hafner
items.authorName: Danijar Hafner · 世界模型 / Dreamer
items.title: Learning to Model the World with Language
items.textEn:
Learning to Model the World with Language

To interact with humans and act in the world, agents need to understand the range of language that people use and relate it to the visual world. While current agents can learn to execute simple language instructions, we aim to build agents that leverage diverse language -- language like "this button turns on the TV" or "I put the bowls away" -- that conveys general knowledge, describes the state of the world, provides interactive feedback, and more. Our key idea is that agents should interpret such diverse language as a signal that helps them predict the future: what they will observe, how the world will behave, and which situations will be rewarded. This perspective unifies language understanding with future prediction as a powerful self-supervised learning objective. We instantiate this in Dynalang, an agent that learns a multimodal world model to predict future text and image representations, and learns to act from imagined model rollouts. While current methods that learn language-conditioned policies degrade in performance with more diverse types of language, we show that Dynalang learns to leverage environment descriptions, game rules, and instructions to excel on tasks ranging from game-playing to navigating photorealistic home scans. Finally, we show that our method enables additional capabilities due to learning a generative model: Dynalang can be pretrained on text-only data, enabling learning from offline datasets, and generate language grounded in an environment.

items.author: Andrej Karpathy
items.title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero（Karpathy 从零到精通）
items.textEn:
Type: course
Andrej Karpathy's free video course: from hand-coding backpropagation (micrograd) all the way to building GPT from scratch (nanoGPT), writing code live while explaining the principles. A classic path from zero to reading frontier-model code, with all companion code open-sourced.
items.summaryZh: Andrej Karpathy 的免费视频课程：从手写反向传播 (micrograd) 一路讲到自己实现 GPT (nanoGPT)，每节课都边写代码边讲原理。零基础到能读懂大模型代码的经典入门路径，配套代码全部开源。

items.author: wm-dreamerv3-2023
items.title: Mastering Diverse Domains through World Models（DreamerV3）
items.textEn:
Venue: Nature 2025
Authors: Hafner et al.
DreamerV3: a single world-model algorithm with fixed hyperparameters that succeeds across 150+ tasks (including collecting diamonds from scratch in Minecraft with no human data), showing the generality and scalability of world-model methods. A key step toward the 'foundation model for control' narrative.
items.summaryZh: DreamerV3：一套固定超参的世界模型算法横跨 150+ 任务（含从零在 Minecraft 中挖到钻石，无需人类数据），展示了世界模型方法的通用性与可扩展性。是「通用控制基础模型」叙事的关键一步。

items.author: wm-daydreamer-2022
items.title: DayDreamer：用世界模型让真实机器人快速在线学习
items.textEn:
Venue: CoRL 2022
Authors: Wu et al.
DayDreamer: applies Dreamer directly on real physical robots, letting quadrupeds, arms and others learn locomotion and manipulation online within hours and without simulation. A strong real-world validation of the sample-efficiency advantage of world models — highly relevant to robotics deployment.
items.summaryZh: DayDreamer：把 Dreamer 直接搬到真实物理机器人上，让四足、机械臂等在数小时内、无需仿真即可在线学会运动与操作技能。世界模型「样本效率高」这一优势在真机上的有力验证，对机器人落地极具参考意义。

items.author: wm-director-2022
items.title: Director：在世界模型中进行分层规划
items.textEn:
Venue: NeurIPS 2022
Authors: Hafner et al.
Director: hierarchical reinforcement learning inside the latent space of a learned world model — a high-level manager sets subgoals and a low-level worker executes them, solving sparse-reward tasks that require long-horizon exploration. A representative combination of world models with hierarchical decision-making.
items.summaryZh: Director：在学到的世界模型隐空间里做分层强化学习——高层管理者设定子目标、低层工人执行，从而解决需要长程探索的稀疏奖励任务。把世界模型与分层决策结合的代表作。

items.author: wm-dreamerv2-2020
items.title: Mastering Atari with Discrete World Models（DreamerV2）
items.textEn:
Venue: ICLR 2021
Authors: Hafner et al.
DreamerV2: a world model with discrete latent variables that, for the first time, lets an agent trained purely inside the world model reach human-level performance on Atari — on a single GPU. Demonstrates the effectiveness of discrete representations for world models.
items.summaryZh: DreamerV2：用离散隐变量的世界模型，在 Atari 上首次让「纯在世界模型里训练」的智能体达到人类水平，且只用单块 GPU。证明了离散表征对世界模型的有效性。

items.author: wm-dreamer-2019
items.title: Dream to Control（Dreamer）：在隐空间想象中学习行为
items.textEn:
Venue: ICLR 2020
Authors: Hafner et al.
Dreamer: learns behaviors by 'imagining' long-horizon trajectories inside a learned latent world model and propagating analytic value gradients back through it, learning policy and value functions efficiently. Substantially outperforms prior methods using only the world model; establishes the framework carried into DreamerV2/V3.
items.summaryZh: Dreamer：在学到的隐空间世界模型里「想象」长程轨迹，并通过可微分的价值反传来高效学习策略与价值函数。在仅用世界模型的情况下大幅超越此前方法，奠定了 DreamerV2/V3 的整体框架。

items.author: sr05-paths-value-learning
items.title: A Paths Perspective on Value Learning（价值学习的路径视角）
items.textEn:
Venue: Distill 2019 · Saturday Robotics Club 05
Reading Club 05 selection. A Distill visual explainer that uses a 'paths' perspective to build intuition for how value functions are learned and propagated in reinforcement learning.
items.summaryZh: Saturday Robotics 世界模型读书会 Club 05 选读（Meta FAIR 主讲「面向物理智能的世界模型」）。Distill 经典可视化长文，用「路径」视角直观解释强化学习中价值函数的学习与传播过程。理解基于模型的规划与价值学习的入门读物。

items.author: wm-planet-2018
items.title: PlaNet：从像素学习的隐空间动力学规划
items.textEn:
Venue: ICML 2019
Authors: Hafner et al.
PlaNet (Deep Planning Network): learns a latent-space dynamics model directly from pixels and plans in that latent space, solving continuous-control tasks with far fewer environment interactions than model-free RL. Pushes 'learn dynamics + plan' to high-dimensional visual input; the direct precursor to Dreamer.
items.summaryZh: Deep Planning Network (PlaNet)：直接从像素学习一个隐空间的环境动力学模型，并在隐空间中做规划，用远少于无模型 RL 的交互次数完成连续控制任务。把「学动力学 + 规划」推进到高维视觉输入，是 Dreamer 的前身。

items.author: Jay Alammar
items.title: The Illustrated Transformer（图解 Transformer）
items.textEn:
Type: article
Jay Alammar's classic illustrated explainer that uses heavy visualization to make the Transformer's attention mechanism and encoder-decoder structure clear. Almost unavoidable as an entry point for understanding modern large-model architecture, readable without a math background.
items.summaryZh: Jay Alammar 的经典图解长文，用大量可视化把 Transformer 的注意力机制、编码器-解码器结构讲清楚。几乎是理解现代大模型架构绕不开的入门读物，无需数学背景也能读懂主干。

items.author: wm-ha-schmidhuber-2018
items.title: World Models（世界模型 · 开山之作）
items.textEn:
Venue: NeurIPS 2018
Authors: David Ha, Jürgen Schmidhuber
Foundational world-models paper (Ha & Schmidhuber): an agent learns a compressed generative 'world model' (VAE + RNN) of its environment, then trains its controller inside that imagined model rather than the real environment. Introduces the 'train by dreaming inside the model' paradigm that the later Dreamer line builds on. The starting point for understanding the embodied-AI / robotics 'world model' narrative.
items.summaryZh: 世界模型领域的奠基论文：智能体先学一个对环境的压缩生成式「世界模型」(VAE + RNN)，再在这个「想象」出来的模型里训练控制策略，而非直接在真实环境里试错。提出了「在模型内做梦式训练」的范式，是后续 Dreamer 系列的思想源头。理解具身智能与机器人「世界模型」叙事的起点。
